title: Sliding Window Maximum
problem_statement: Return the maximum value in each sliding window of size k over an array.

examples.explanation: Each window maximum is computed as the window slides right by one position.

intuition: Use a deque to keep candidate elements in decreasing order so the maximum is always at the front.

approaches.name: Monotonic deque
approaches.description: Maintain indices of elements in a deque, removing smaller elements from the back and expired indices from the front.
approaches.why_not: Computing maximum for each window independently is O(nk) and too slow.

interview_qa.q: Why store indices instead of values?
interview_qa.a: Indices allow removing elements that move out of the window.
Values in the deque are in decreasing order from front to back.
